steps.title: Use the y-intercept from the table
steps.content: At $x=0$, the table gives $y=18$. For $y = mx + b$, the y-intercept is $b = 18$. This already eliminates options A and C (both have $b=13$).

steps.title: Compute the slope between two table points
Check each choice
steps.content:
• A. $y=18x+13$ — wrong slope and intercept; eliminated.
  • B. $y=18x+18$ — slope $+18$ doesn't fit the data; eliminated.
  • C. $y=-5x+13$ — correct slope but $y(0)=13$, not 18; eliminated.
  • D. $y=-5x+18$ — matches both the slope and intercept.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
